items.q: Which major African river crosses the Equator twice during its course?
items.explanation: The Congo River curves across central Africa and is the only major river known for crossing the Equator twice.

items.q: Lesotho is entirely surrounded by South Africa.
items.explanation: Lesotho is one of the world's few sovereign enclaves because every part of its border touches South Africa.

items.q: The Sahara Desert has been shrinking in recent decades.
items.explanation: Research has shown that the Sahara has expanded over some modern periods, especially when compared with the early twentieth century.

items.q: What is a mesa in physical geography?
options: A mesa is an isolated flat-topped landform with steep sides.
A basin is a broad low area drained by a river system.
A fjord is a narrow sea inlet cut by glacial erosion.
A dune ridge is a long ridge of sand deposited by coastal winds.
items.a: A mesa is an isolated flat-topped landform with steep sides.
items.explanation: Mesas commonly form in dry regions where harder rock protects the top while the surrounding land erodes away.

items.q: Which river is the longest in Asia and flows entirely within China?
items.explanation: The Yangtze runs from the Tibetan Plateau to the East China Sea and is the longest river in Asia.

items.q: Which lake on the Peru-Bolivia border is commonly described as the highest commercially navigable lake in the world?
items.explanation: Lake Titicaca lies high in the Andes and has long supported trade, farming, and settlement on both sides of the border.

options: A caldera is a large volcanic depression formed after part of a volcano collapses.
A delta is a flat coastal plain built by river sediment.
An endorheic basin is a dry basin with no outlet to the sea.
A strait is a narrow channel connecting two larger seas.
items.a: A caldera is a large volcanic depression formed after part of a volcano collapses.
items.explanation: Calderas often form after major eruptions empty part of a magma chamber and the ground above it sinks.

items.q: Which African country has three capital cities?
items.explanation: Pretoria serves as the executive capital, Cape Town as the legislative capital, and Bloemfontein as the judicial capital.

items.q: What is the world's highest uninterrupted waterfall?
items.explanation: Angel Falls in Venezuela drops from the Auyan-tepui and is the world's tallest uninterrupted waterfall.

options: An isthmus is a narrow strip of land connecting two larger landmasses.
A basin is a low area of land where a river and its tributaries drain.
A plateau is a broad area of high flat land.
A fjord is a long narrow sea inlet with steep sides.
items.a: An isthmus is a narrow strip of land connecting two larger landmasses.
items.explanation: The Isthmus of Panama is a classic example because it links North and South America while separating the Atlantic and Pacific.
